label: reconstruction_v3_foundation

scope.purpose: physics-informed reconstruction from raw DPFI or DFFI camera counts without requiring a TF morphology
scope.status: shape-flexible synthetic development contract; experimental calibration and validation remain pending
scope.absolute_faraday_scale: conditional on kappa_F until experimentally calibrated

smooth_reference.name: smooth_projected_tf_free_radii
smooth_reference.interpretation: low-dimensional reference and diagnostic model; not a contact-only claim for the real dipolar condensate

shape_flexible_baseline.name: nonnegative_bilinear_finite_support
shape_flexible_baseline.knot_grid: fixed before fitting and no finer than the supported optical information

regularisation.interpretation: the same physical curvature prior is comparable across knot spacings; the regularisation density scale is independent of optimiser scaling

shape_flexible_baseline.interpretation: resolved asymmetric and multi-peak density without imposing a TF envelope

parameter_bounds.provenance: broad provisional physical ranges independent of synthetic truth; replace with apparatus and data-family ranges before real-data reporting

synthetic_morphology_benchmark.truth_source: analytic maps generated independently of the bilinear fitting basis

initialisation.dual_port: raw-count nonnegative linearisation at zero density
initialisation.dark_field: square-root intensity moments followed by projection of a smooth seed onto the flexible basis

synthetic_morphology_benchmark.interpretation: controlled reconstruction stress tests, not equilibrium predictions for 166Er

ensemble_selection_contract.noise_draws: generate once and reuse identical morphology, realization and seed keys for every candidate
ensemble_selection_contract.near_equivalent_tie_break: fewest fitted coefficients, then error, then label
ensemble_selection_contract.held_out_rule: freeze the selected candidate and reject any morphology name used during calibration

required_calibration_contract: read noise per pixel and readout
pupil or measured optical transfer function
Faraday response coefficient for absolute density
known_boundaries: dark-field intensity does not measure rotation sign
kappa_F and density amplitude are structurally degenerate without calibration
local Jacobian errors are diagnostics rather than final confidence intervals
IRLS remains to be benchmarked against a mixed Poisson-Gaussian likelihood
matched-model synthetic recovery is not experimental validation
regularisation cannot restore spatial frequencies removed by the aperture
